_meta.source: Guttmacher Institute, State Bans on Abortion Throughout Pregnancy (guttmacher.org/state-policy/explore/state-policies-abortion-bans, updated 2026-06-04); KFF Abortion in the U.S. Dashboard (kff.org/womens-health-policy/abortion-in-the-u-s-dashboard, map dated 2026-03-09)
_meta.notes: Gestational-limit based classification: 0 = banned (total or near-total ban, effectively ~6 weeks or less, or no broad exceptions), 1 = restricted (legal with a gestational limit roughly 6-22 weeks), 2 = legal/protected (legal to fetal viability ~24+ weeks, no gestational limit, or codified state constitutional/statutory protection). This is a fast-moving area of law; several states are mid-litigation or facing ballot measures that could change their classification on short notice. Flagged as contested/volatile as of 2026-07: MO — legal to viability under a 2024 constitutional amendment, but a repeal measure reinstating a near-total ban is on the 2026-11-03 ballot; OH — constitutional viability protection (2023) is controlling, but a conflicting statutory 20-week ban remains on the books and unresolved; WY — total ban struck down by the state supreme court (2026-01), a replacement 6-week ban is currently enjoined pending litigation, so practical status has reverted to a pre-existing viability standard but could change abruptly. Verify current status for these three before relying on this data.
